title: Pentacalia sotarensis
taxonomy: Pentacalia sotarensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, and it belongs to the genus Pentacalia. This plant is native to the Andes mountains in South America, specifically in Colombia and Ecuador. Pentacalia sotarensis is classified within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. It is further classified in the order Asterales and family Asteraceae, with the order and family being its most specific classification. Pentacalia sotarensis is a rare plant species.

This plant has been traditionally used in Ecuadorian folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
